verdict: PASS — all 11 checks clear. No orphan bibliography entries, no orphan markers. All other checks continue to pass.

format_discipline.notes: First non-empty line is the H1 title. No leading YAML block. Meta sidecar exists and parses (field names vary slightly from SKILL spec — reading_minutes vs estimated_read_minutes, resources_cited vs citations_used — but all data is present; not a hard fail). length.notes: 825 words is within the 600–900 standard depth range. Read-aloud at 230 wpm ≈ 3.6 min; word count is the authoritative gate and it passes.

citations.notes: [3] now cited inline at line 50: 'Scalars are the atom; those structures are built from them [3].' — the name-and-defer pointer to vectors/matrices. [1] cited at lines 11, 20, 34, 42, 48, 64. [2] cited at lines 40, 42, 68, 72, 74. All three markers appear inline and in References. No orphans in either direction.

no_forward_references.notes: Vectors (6.3) and matrices (6.4) each appear in exactly one name-and-defer sentence with explicit topic-number pointers and zero instructional content about how they work. Key Takeaways line 83 also names-and-defers correctly. No forward topic is taught.

faithfulness.notes: All 10 sampled assertions trace to corpus §4–§8: scalar definition, temperature/battery/star-rating examples, magnitude definition, loss and learning rate explanations, one-element list distinction, config file worked example, three In Practice roles, and naming best practice all match corpus text verbatim or by close paraphrase.

beginner_readability.notes: All non-everyday terms (scalar, magnitude, loss, learning rate) are bolded and defined in plain language at first use. Every concept leads with a concrete everyday example before the abstraction. No hedging phrases detected. Average sentence length well under 25 words.

formatting.notes: All prose paragraphs are 2–4 sentences. Bullet lists and a table used for multi-item content (everyday scalars, AI scalars, In Practice roles). Inline examples appear in Key Concepts and In Practice, not only in Worked Example. Bold-term/plain-meaning pattern applied consistently.

no_assessment_references.notes: No assessment IDs, due-week references, or grade weights appear anywhere in the reading.

scope.notes: Reading stays squarely on the headline topic throughout. Vectors and matrices appear only as named-and-deferred pointers with explicit topic numbers. No vector or matrix mechanics are taught.
